evals.prompt: I want to add a notifications feature to my app. Users should see a list of notifications on their phone and a summary on their Wear OS watch. Scaffold the feature and set up the basic structure.
evals.expected_output: Uses scaffold-feature.sh with --wear flag, creates correct module structure with platform submodules, follows naming conventions

assertions.text: Uses the scaffold-feature.sh script (not manual file creation) to generate the feature
Passes the --wear flag to scaffold-feature.sh since the feature targets both phone and Wear OS
Feature is created under features/notifications/ with both app/ and wear/ submodules
Does not create a flat feature module (no features/notifications/src/ without platform submodules)
Mentions registering Koin DI modules in the platform's DI setup as a follow-up step
Mentions adding navigation routes in the platform modules as a follow-up step
Mentions adding strings in :core with English and Spanish translations
Does not add dependencies between feature modules

evals.prompt: I need a use case in :core that fetches the user's workout history from the repository and returns it sorted by date. Create the GetWorkoutHistory use case.
evals.expected_output: Uses scaffold-usecase.sh, places use case in :core, follows use case pattern with Result<T>, wires DI

assertions.text: Uses the scaffold-usecase.sh script to generate the use case boilerplate
Use case is placed in :core (core/domain/usecase/), not in a feature module
Use case returns Result<T> (not raw types or nullable types)
Use case takes a repository as a constructor parameter (dependency injection)
Mentions registering the use case in Koin core DI module with factory { GetWorkoutHistoryUseCase(get()) }
Does not put business logic in a feature module
Mentions writing a test for the use case

evals.prompt: I have a data class called WorkoutSummary that's used by both the :features:dashboard and :features:profile features. Right now it's in features/dashboard/app/. Where should I move it?
evals.expected_output: Recommends moving to :core:domain:model, explains features can't depend on each other, only on :core

assertions.text: Recommends moving WorkoutSummary to :core (specifically core/domain/model/)
Explains that feature modules cannot depend on each other — only on :core
Does not suggest adding a dependency between :features:dashboard and :features:profile
Does not suggest creating a new shared module between features (the answer is :core)
Mentions that Gradle enforces this compile-time boundary

evals.prompt: We already have a :features:dashboard:app module for phone. I want to add watch support so users can see their dashboard on Wear OS too. Walk me through what I need to do.
evals.expected_output: Creates wear/ submodule alongside app/, uses Wear Material 3, separate ViewModel, wires in :wear module

assertions.text: Creates a wear/ submodule alongside the existing app/ submodule (features/dashboard/wear/)
Wear submodule gets its own ViewModel (e.g., WearDashboardViewModel), not sharing the phone ViewModel
Uses Wear Material 3 components (androidx.wear.compose.material3), not regular Material 3
Creates a Wear-specific Koin DI module for the feature
Mentions wiring navigation in the :wear platform module (not in the feature module)
States that business logic in :core is already shared — no changes needed there
Does not suggest sharing UI or ViewModels between app/ and wear/ submodules

evals.prompt: I want to add a home screen widget that shows today's step count. Set it up for me.
evals.expected_output: Creates :widget module at root level, depends only on :core, uses Glance or AppWidgetProvider, does not put code in :app

assertions.text: Creates a :widget module at root level (widget/), not inside :app or a feature module
Widget module depends only on :core, not on :app, :wear, or any feature module
Uses Glance (Compose-based) or AppWidgetProvider for the widget implementation
Accesses step count data through a use case or repository from :core
Does not put widget code inside the :app module
Mentions adding the widget module to settings.gradle.kts

evals.prompt: I need a new settings feature — just for the phone app, no watch support needed. Users can toggle dark mode and change their language. Set up the feature module.
evals.expected_output: Uses scaffold-feature.sh WITHOUT --wear, still creates app/ submodule (not flat), adds strings_settings.xml in :core

assertions.text: Uses scaffold-feature.sh without the --wear flag (phone-only feature)
Creates features/settings/app/ submodule, NOT a flat features/settings/src/ structure
Does NOT create a features/settings/wear/ submodule since Wear support was not requested
Uses Koin for DI (not Hilt/Dagger)
Uses StateFlow in the ViewModel (not LiveData)
Adds strings in :core as strings_settings.xml (not in the feature module's own resources)
Mentions that adding Wear support later is just adding a wear/ sibling submodule

evals.prompt: We built :features:feed as a phone-only feature 3 months ago. It has its own ViewModel, screens, and some string resources in the feature's app/ module. Now the PM wants the feed on Wear OS too. Walk me through the full refactor — what needs to move, what stays, and what's new.
evals.expected_output: Moves strings from feature to :core as strings_feed.xml, creates wear/ submodule with separate ViewModel, keeps core logic untouched, explains the string migration specifically

assertions.text: Identifies that string resources currently in features/feed/app/res/ need to move to :core as strings_feed.xml
Creates features/feed/wear/ submodule alongside existing features/feed/app/
Wear submodule gets its own ViewModel (not sharing the phone ViewModel)
Uses Wear Material 3 components, not regular Material 3
States that business logic in :core does not need changes
Explains why strings must move to :core (shared across platforms) rather than duplicating them
Mentions updating the phone feature's code to reference the moved string resources
Does not suggest sharing ViewModels or UI between app/ and wear/

evals.prompt: I'm fetching articles from the API and caching them in Room. Should this caching logic live in the use case (GetArticlesUseCase) or in the repository (ArticleRepositoryImpl)? Also, after fetching I need to filter out articles the user has already read — where does that filtering go?
evals.expected_output: Caching in repository (data layer concern), filtering read articles in use case (business rule). Explains the boundary between data and domain layers in this architecture.

assertions.text: Places caching logic (fetch from API + store in Room) in the repository, not in the use case
Places the 'filter out read articles' logic in the use case, not in the repository
Explains why: caching is a data-layer concern (how data is stored/retrieved), while filtering by read-status is a business rule (what data the user should see)
Repository method signature returns raw data without business filtering applied
Use case follows the project pattern: suspend operator fun invoke() returning kotlin.Result<T> (not a custom wrapper type)

evals.prompt: I need to add a Wear OS complication that shows today's step count on the user's watch face. How should I set this up?
evals.expected_output: Creates :complications module at root level, depends only on :core, uses SuspendingComplicationDataSourceService, does not put code in :wear

assertions.text: Creates a :complications module at root level (complications/), not inside :wear or a feature module
Complications module depends only on :core, not on :wear, :app, or any feature module
Uses SuspendingComplicationDataSourceService to serve complication data
Accesses step count data through a use case or repository from :core
Does not put complication code inside the :wear module
Mentions adding the complications module to settings.gradle.kts with include(":complications")
Explains that complications are standalone OS entry points the watch face calls directly, outside the app's UI
